text:
There was a fish named Ferg. One morning Ferg woke up and realized something was terribly wrong. He was surrounded by piles and piles of garbage. Everywhere he looked there were plastic bottles, Styrofoam, trash bags, and old fast-food wrappers.

Desperate for help, Ferg began to swim around anxiously. As he did, he noticed a tiny hole up ahead through which a butterfly-like moth was fluttering its delicate wings. It was fluttering so fast that it appeared to be carrying stuff away.

With determination, Ferg figured out how to reach through the hole. Inch by fragile inch, he gathered handfuls of garbage until he had enough. He tossed them out of the hole. Each time he did, the moth collected more debris.

Just then, another insect joined the moth—an entire family—fluttering about with bags and wrappers.

Ferg told them about his situation. They laughed, thinking his attempts futile.

With surprising strength, Ferg tossed another bag
